marketing.tagline: Co-parenting communication, made calmer and clearer.
marketing.subtitle: Copara helps separated parents message neutrally, manage parenting schedules, track shared expenses, and keep organized records without turning every conversation into a conflict.

auth.magicLinkSent: Check your email for a sign-in link.
auth.confirmEmailSent: Check your email to confirm your account, then sign in.

onboarding.circleSubtitle: A circle is the shared space you and your co-parent will use together.
onboarding.inviteSubtitle: They'll get an email with a link to join — no app install required to respond.
onboarding.childrenSubtitle: Medical notes, school info, and more can be added later from the Vault.

join.title: You've been invited to {circleName}
join.subtitle: Copara helps separated parents communicate neutrally, coordinate schedules, and keep organized tamper-evident records.
join.invalid: This invite is no longer valid.
join.signUpFirst: Create an account to accept this invite.

messages.title: Messages
messages.emptyDescription: Start a neutral, documented conversation with your co-parent. Every message is timestamped and tamper-evident.
messages.attachmentTooLarge: Each file must be 25 MB or smaller.
messages.attachmentType: Only images, PDFs, and Word documents are allowed.
messages.offlineBanner: You're offline. Text messages will be queued and sent when you reconnect.
messages.offlineNoAttachments: Attachments can't be sent offline. Try again when you're back online.
messages.queuedBanner: {count, plural, one {# message queued to send} other {# messages queued to send}}

calendar.title: Calendar
calendar.checkInNoLocation: Location access is required to check in.
calendar.scheduleTemplatesHint: Set up a recurring custody pattern and optionally generate events for the next 4 weeks.
calendar.needTwoParents: Both parents must be active in the circle to configure a schedule.
calendar.applyNow: Generate events for the next 4 weeks

expenses.title: Expenses
expenses.receiptType: Receipt must be an image or PDF.

exports.title: Exports
exports.subtitle: Generate tamper-evident PDF records with hash-chain verification.
exports.summarySubtitle: Summarize conflicts with message-ID citations. Not legal advice.
exports.verifyDisclaimer: Tamper-evident record export — not certified or court-admitted.
exports.tamperDetected: Tamper detected: live records no longer match this export digest.

pro.title: Professional dashboard
pro.signInPrompt: Sign in to manage your co-parenting cases.
pro.setupSubtitle: Create a client case to invite both parents, or share your referral link for families signing up on their own.
pro.dashboardSubtitle: Open a client case to review records and generate exports.
pro.step1Body: Click New case, name the matter, and enter both parents' email addresses. Copara sends each parent an invite.
pro.step2Body: Both parents use the same dual-parent link. Each signs in with the email that received the invite.
pro.step3Body: Once parents join, open the case to view permitted records and generate organized exports.
pro.step4Body: After accepting the invite, each parent starts a {trialDays}-day free trial, then subscribes to use Copara. Partner access stays free.
pro.caseInviteToolWhen: Use for active client matters you are managing.
pro.caseInviteToolPoint1: Creates a shared circle and emails both parents.
pro.caseInviteToolPoint2: Gives you read-only access to messages, calendar, and exports.
pro.caseInviteToolPoint3: Does not automatically track referral bonuses.
pro.referralLinkToolWhen: Use when a family signs up on their own, outside a case you created.
pro.referralLinkToolPoint1: Attributes sign-ups to you for bonus tracking.
pro.referralLinkToolPoint2: Earn a bonus when they subscribe (one per household).
pro.referralLinkToolPoint3: Does not create a case or grant you record access.
pro.billingBody: Your partner account is free. Parents subscribe after joining — {trialDays}-day free trial first. A Family Circle plan can cover both parents in one subscription.
pro.accessBody: Read-only by default. You can view permitted records and run exports, but you cannot send messages or act as a parent in the app.
pro.statusInvited: Invite sent — parent has not accepted yet.
pro.statusActive: Parent joined the case. They may still need to complete subscription before using the app.
pro.statusProfessional: You — the partner on this case.
pro.benefitReadOnlyBody: View messages, calendar, and expenses where parents grant access — without altering records or messaging as a parent.
pro.benefitFollowUpBody: Manage cases from one dashboard. Track membership status and stay current on client activity.
pro.benefitMaterialsBody: Download handouts, email templates, and brand assets from the Marketing materials section on your dashboard.
pro.benefitReferralBody: Share your referral link and earn a bonus when referred families subscribe. Track status in your dashboard.
pro.caseBillingBody: Accepting the case invite does not waive billing. Each parent starts a {trialDays}-day free trial, then chooses a plan. You can remind them — subscription status is not shown here.
pro.setupBillingTitle: Parents pay; partner access is free
pro.setupBillingBody: After parents accept the invite, they start a {trialDays}-day free trial and then subscribe. Your dashboard access remains free.
pro.memberStatusHint: invited = not yet joined · active = account linked
pro.createFirstCaseHint: Use New case to open the setup form. Enter the case name and both parents' emails — we'll send invites automatically.
pro.yourCasesHint: Select a case to view members, share the invite link, and run exports.
pro.noCasesHint: You haven't created a case yet.
pro.referralProgramHint: Track sign-ups and bonuses from families who use your /r/ link.

partnerProfile.title: Your practice
partnerProfile.description: Add your company name and referral link. The link path is suggested from your company — edit it if you prefer something shorter.
partnerProfile.companyHint: Shown on your partner profile and used to suggest your referral link.
partnerProfile.slugHint: Letters, numbers, and hyphens only (3–48 characters). Updates automatically when you change your company name unless you edit this field.

pro.newCaseDescription: Invite both parents with one dual-parent link. Each signs in with the email that received the invite.
pro.casePageDescription: Share the invite link with both parents, then generate organized exports when they join.
pro.dualInviteHint: Share this single link with both parents. Each signs in with the email that received the invite.
pro.clientHandoutHint: PDF with this case invite link and QR code — give to clients after creating the case.
pro.invalidCase: This case link is no longer valid.
pro.caseInviteSubtitle: Your mediator invited both parents to Copara. Sign in with the email address that received the invite.
pro.allAccepted: Both parents have already joined this case.

materials.sectionDescription: Download digital handouts and copy email templates to share with clients. PDFs appear here once added to the partner kit.
materials.downloadsIntro: PDFs and brand assets for your practice. Generated PDFs are ready to download; the logo pack appears when published.
materials.englishFallback: English version (French PDF not yet available).
materials.emailTemplatesIntro: Copy and personalize before sending. Case invites are also sent automatically when you create a case.
materials.emailCaseDescription: Optional follow-up if a parent did not receive the automatic invite.
materials.emailCaseBody:
Subject: Your Copara case invite

Hi,

I've set up a co-parenting case for us on Copara — a shared space for messages, calendar, expenses, and organized records.

You should receive an email from Copara with a link to join. Please sign in with this email address ({trialDays}-day free trial, then a subscription).

If you don't see the email, check spam or let me know and I can resend the invite link from my partner dashboard.

Best,
materials.emailReferralDescription: For families signing up on their own — not tied to a case you created.
materials.emailReferralBody:
Subject: Copara for co-parenting

Hi,

I use Copara with client families for calmer co-parenting communication and organized records. If you'd like to try it, you can sign up here:

{referralUrl}

Both parents can share one Family Circle subscription after a {trialDays}-day free trial.

Best,

clientHandout.description: Download from each case page — PDF includes that case's invite link and QR code.

logoPack.title: Logo pack (ZIP)
logoPack.description: Copara logos and marks for slide decks and websites.

vault.emptyDescription: Store medical info, school details, emergency contacts, and documents for each child — with sharing controls for professionals.

vault.addChildTitle: Add a child to get started
vault.addChildHint: Vault info is organized per child. Add at least one child to store medical and school notes.
vault.fileType: File must be an image, PDF, or Word document.
vault.shareWithProfessional: Share with professionals in this circle
vault.circleDocumentsHint: Documents not linked to a specific child.

journal.subtitle: Circle updates for both parents
journal.empty: No journal entries yet. Share the first update.
journal.entryPlaceholder: Share news, a funny quote, or a moment from your week…
journal.mediaTooLarge: Each file must be under 25 MB.
journal.mediaType: Files must be images or video (MP4/MOV).

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These Terms govern your use of Copara, a co-parenting coordination service operated for separated parents and the professionals who support them.

sections.title: Service description
paragraphs: Copara provides messaging, scheduling, expense tracking, record exports, and AI-assisted tools. Copara is not a law firm, mediator, or court. Nothing in the service constitutes legal advice.
Exports are tamper-evident, timestamped records suitable for review by legal professionals. They are not certified, notarized, or guaranteed to be admissible in any proceeding.

sections.title: Accounts and circles
paragraphs: You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials and for activity under your account.
A circle may include up to two parent members plus invited third parties or professionals, subject to sharing controls set by parents.

sections.title: Acceptable use
paragraphs: You agree not to use Copara to harass, threaten, or impersonate others, or to upload unlawful content.
We may suspend accounts that violate these Terms or pose a safety risk to other users.

sections.title: AI features
paragraphs: Tone review and dispute summaries are AI-generated assistive tools. You remain responsible for messages you send.
AI outputs may be inaccurate. Always review suggestions before sending or sharing with third parties.

sections.title: Limitation of liability
paragraphs: Copara is provided "as is" to the fullest extent permitted by applicable law. We are not liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from your use of the service.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Copara respects your privacy. This policy describes how we collect, use, and protect personal information, including for users in Canada subject to PIPEDA.

sections.title: Information we collect
paragraphs: Account information: name, email address, and profile preferences.

Co-parenting records: messages, calendar events, expenses, check-in confirmations (location verified as true/false only — raw GPS coordinates are never stored or shared), attachments, and exports you generate.
Technical data: device push subscription endpoints, service logs, and analytics events such as PWA install funnel steps.

sections.title: How we use information
paragraphs: We use your information to operate the service, deliver notifications, maintain tamper-evident records, and improve reliability.
AI features process content server-side. We store hashed inputs and structured outputs in audit logs rather than retaining raw drafts indefinitely unless required for the feature you requested.

sections.title: Sharing
paragraphs: Information in your circle is shared with active circle members according to role and sharing settings. Professionals receive read-only access only to content parents choose to share.

We use subprocessors including Supabase (database, auth, storage), OpenAI (AI processing), Resend (email), and Vercel (hosting). Data may be processed in the United States unless otherwise configured.

sections.title: Canadian users (PIPEDA)
paragraphs: If you are in Canada, you have the right to access, correct, and withdraw consent to non-essential processing of your personal information, subject to legal and contractual restrictions.

We collect only information reasonably necessary for the identified purposes above. You may contact us to request access or deletion, understanding that some records may need to be retained where required by law or legitimate business needs (e.g., audit logs).

sections.title: Retention and security
paragraphs: Messages and financial records are designed to be append-only for integrity. Row-level security in our database restricts access to circle members.
We use encryption in transit and industry-standard safeguards. No method of transmission or storage is 100% secure.

sections.title: Contact
paragraphs: Privacy questions or PIPEDA requests: contact Copara at the support email configured for your deployment.

pwa.installSubtitle: Get faster access and notifications on your home screen.
pwa.updateTitle: A new version is available — refresh
